name: Rome (Italy)

colors.symbolism: the imperial purple of ancient Rome
the gold (or) of the city's heraldry

description: A vertical bicolour of dark crimson-purple at the hoist and gold at the fly.
history: Adopted by the Capitoline Council in 1864; the colours derive from the medieval and Renaissance livery of the city, themselves a memory of the imperial purple and gold of ancient Rome. The municipal coat of arms (a red shield bearing the gold letters SPQR beneath a crown) is often added when the flag is used officially.

features.symbolism: the historic city colours, a memory of the imperial purple and gold of ancient Rome
